bleeding.title: Severe bleeding
steps: Apply firm direct pressure with a clean cloth or bandage.
Keep the injured area elevated if this does not worsen pain.
Do not remove soaked dressings; add more layers over them.
Call 112 or arrange urgent medical transport for heavy bleeding.

burn.title: Burns
steps: Cool the burn under cool running water for 20 minutes.
Remove jewelry or tight items near the burn if they are not stuck.
Cover loosely with a clean, non-fluffy dressing.
Do not apply ice, toothpaste, butter, or oily home remedies.

fracture.title: Suspected fracture
steps: Keep the injured part still and supported.
Do not try to force the bone back into place.
Apply a cold pack wrapped in cloth to reduce swelling.
Seek urgent medical care for severe pain, deformity, or numbness.

cpr.title: CPR
steps: Call 112 and ask for an ambulance immediately.
Start hard, fast chest compressions in the center of the chest.
If trained, give 30 compressions and 2 rescue breaths.
Continue until trained responders arrive or the person breathes normally.
